verdict.answer_first: A: gives the command immediately after 'On macOS:' with 'lsof -ti :8080 | xargs kill'; B does the same, but adds extra paths later.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to one main command plus one fallback; B dilutes it with 'Quick variants' and 'Want me to run it?'
verdict.fabrication: B adds unsupported/possibly wrong extras: 'One-liner with guard' and 'Newer macOS / Linux alt'; A also makes an unverified claim: 'xargs runs no command'.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives the command, force option, and inspect-first command without extra branches.
